what:
OpenFn adaptor for Twilio. The adaptor lets OpenFn workflows read from and write to Twilio Communications API.

It exposes 1 operation that you can call from a workflow step, listed in the Operations tab. 4 ready-to-paste snippets are grouped as create (4). Vendor homepage: https://www.twilio.com.
connect:
Twilio supports HTTP Basic auth for API access.

The credential schema asks for accountSid (required) and authToken (required). Point the credential at the base URL: https://api.twilio.com/2010-04-01. Auth reference: https://www.twilio.com/docs/usage/api.
when:
Reach for this adaptor when your workflow needs to read or write data in Twilio Communications API.

3 triggers are catalogued (0 webhook, 3 polling), including New Call, New Recording, New SMS. See the Triggers tab for the full list. Twilio can push events to OpenFn via webhooks (docs: https://www.twilio.com/docs/usage/webhooks), so reactive workflows are a good fit.
wire:
A typical wiring pattern is trigger → fetch → transform → write.

Kick the workflow off from a trigger on the Triggers tab (webhook-driven where supported, polling otherwise). Then chain operations from this adaptor's Operations tab to fetch or write data in Twilio. Pass values between steps through state.data — every operation reads from and writes to job state.

To move data between systems, follow this step with another adaptor (e.g. write the fetched records into a downstream FHIR, DHIS2, or database adaptor).
scale:
When running Twilio jobs at volume:

- Prefer Twilio webhooks over polling — they cut latency and rate-limit pressure.
- Paginate list and search calls explicitly; never assume a single response is the full result set.
- Make jobs idempotent by keying on stable external IDs so a replay doesn't create duplicates.
- Surface 4xx/5xx responses with the vendor request ID in your logs, and treat 429s as retries with backoff.

Adapter note: HTTP Basic auth with Account SID + Auth Token; per-product specs (messaging, voice, verify) in twilio-oai.
best:
- Store Twilio credentials in OpenFn's credential store; never inline keys or tokens in job code.
- Pin @openfn/language-twilio to v1.0.13 in package.json so an upstream change doesn't break a production workflow silently.
- Keep each step small — one logical API call per operation makes failures easier to diagnose and retry.
- Add defensive checks before transforming inbound payloads — vendor responses change between versions.
- Log enough context (resource id, run id, vendor request id) so an incident can be traced end-to-end.
